definitions: A data center or point of presence that is part of a distributed network, situated closer to end-users to reduce latency and improve performance.
A facility at the edge of a network, often used for content delivery networks (CDNs) or cloud services, to cache data and serve requests more quickly.
examples: The CDN utilizes multiple edge locations.
Deploying applications to an edge location can enhance user experience.
Data is replicated across various edge locations.
